steps.title: Distribute the negative sign
steps.content:
When we subtract the second polynomial, we distribute the negative sign to each of its terms:
$$(6t^{4}-6t^{2}+4t-2)-(5t^{4}-3t^{3}+7t^{2}-2)$$
$$= 6t^{4}-6t^{2}+4t-2 - 5t^{4} + 3t^{3} - 7t^{2} + 2$$
Notice: $-(5t^{4}-3t^{3}+7t^{2}-2)$ becomes $-5t^{4} + 3t^{3} - 7t^{2} + 2$ as all signs flip.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group terms by degree:
  • $t^4$: $6t^4 - 5t^4 = t^4$
  • $t^3$: $3t^3$
  • $t^2$: $-6t^2 - 7t^2 = -13t^2$
  • $t$: $4t$
  • Constants: $-2 + 2 = 0$
The simplified expression is:
$$t^{4}+3t^{3}-13t^{2}+4t$$That matches choice A — A is correct.
